mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real savings combined with accelerating inflation indicates strong underlying demand outpacing supply, prompting equity market repricing toward higher growth expectations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive Rank IC greater than 0.04 over any 2-year window, or RSAFS and CPIAUCSL diverge without affecting equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply-side recovery, which immediately lifts equity valuations over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain flat or negative during periods of rising INDPRO, or the mechanism shows zero predictive accuracy outside recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 250.0 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 1.5% on average when CPIAUCSL crosses 250.0, or the threshold shows no statistical edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market expansion, supporting higher corporate revenues and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative or insignificant Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows, or payrolls rise while unemployment falls without boosting equities.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts indicate strong residential construction demand and broader economic confidence, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Housing starts data shows no correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the signal produces negative returns in over 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing real GDP growth during late-cycle expansions signals diminishing marginal returns on capital and impending recession risks, driving negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets deliver positive quarterly returns despite decelerating GDPC1, or the hypothesis fails to predict downturns in late-cycle regimes with greater than 60% accuracy.
